mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity capital relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window within zero-bound regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This mechanism operates continuously: each 0.1 percentage point increase in UNRATE predicts a 5 basis point decline in 5-day forward SPX returns through reduced earnings expectations and risk appetite contraction.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 5-day SPX returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value (indicating reversal of predicted direction), or correlation flips sign over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, this indicates demand shock that cascades into earnings downgrades. The interaction effect (joint weakness) is stronger than either signal alone, predicting negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines >2% MoM AND HOUST declines >2% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 535982M and HOUST at 1496K units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS AND HOUST weakness) shows lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC comparison).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins. This predicts negative 60-day forward returns as earnings revisions incorporate capacity underutilization.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k/month AND UNRATE rises simultaneously, this signals labor market inflection point. The interaction (joint deterioration) predicts negative 20-day SPX returns through forward earnings guidance downgrades.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100k AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in prior month
regime_hint: labor market inflection or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on PAYEMS and UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2% signals negative real rates. This regime compresses equity risk premiums and encourages equity allocation over bonds. The mechanism predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as portfolio rebalancing flows into equities and inflation expectations remain anchored.
regime_hint: negative real rates with moderate inflation regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1% with t-test p > 0.10) when trigger is active, or the interaction effect is weaker than either single-series effect alone.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate serves as the primary demand signal. When quarterly GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized, this signals demand weakness that cascades into equity earnings downgrades within 60 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05 in absolute value, or the relationship reverses sign (positive correlation) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both decline simultaneously (INDPRO down >1% MoM AND PAYEMS down >50k in prior month), this signals stagflationary pressure. The interaction predicts negative 5-day returns as equity risk premium expands.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines >1% MoM AND PAYEMS monthly change < -50k
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond INDPRO alone (Rank IC comparison).
